chapter_title.en: HADEETH OF THE SEA WITH THE SUN

text: بَيْنَا مُوسَى بْنُ عِيسَى فِي دَارِهِ الَّتِي فِي الْمَسْعَى يُشْرِفُ عَلَى الْمَسْعَى إِذْ رَأَى أَبَا الْحَسَنِ مُوسَى  ( عليه السلام ) مُقْبِلًا مِنَ الْمَرْوَةِ عَلَى بَغْلَةٍ فَأَمَرَ ابْنُ هَيَّاجٍ رَجُلًا مِنْ هَمْدَانَ مُنْقَطِعاً إِلَيْهِ أَنْ يَتَعَلَّقَ بِلِجَامِهِ وَ يَدَّعِيَ الْبَغْلَةَ فَأَتَاهُ فَتَعَلَّقَ بِاللِّجَامِ وَ ادَّعَى الْبَغْلَةَ فَثَنَى أَبُو الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) رِجْلَهُ فَنَزَلَ عَنْهَا وَ قَالَ لِغِلْمَانِهِ خُذُوا سَرْجَهَا وَ ادْفَعُوهَا إِلَيْهِ فَقَالَ وَ السَّرْجُ أَيْضاً لِي فَقَالَ أَبُو الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) كَذَبْتَ عِنْدَنَا الْبَيِّنَةُ بِأَنَّهُ سَرْجُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ وَ أَمَّا الْبَغْلَةُ فَإِنَّا اشْتَرَيْنَاهَا مُنْذُ قَرِيبٍ وَ أَنْتَ أَعْلَمُ وَ مَا قُلْتَ.

en.hubeali: H 14496 – From him, from Ahmad, from Al-Barqy, from Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Hammaad Bin usmaan who said:

‘Musa Bin Isa was in his house which was in the place of Sa’ee (nearby the Kaaba), overlooking it when he saw Abu Al-Hassan Musaasws coming from Al-Marwa upon hisasws mule. So he ordered Ibn Hayyaaj, a man from Hamdaan who was very close to himasws to go and hold on to the reins and make a claim for the mule. So he came up to himasws, held on to the reins, and staked a claim for the mule. Abu Al-Hassanasws took hisasws feet off and dismounted from it and said to hisasws servant: ‘Take the saddle and hand (the mule) over to him’. He said, ‘And the saddle is mine as well’. So Abu Al-Hassanasws said: ‘You are lying, for weasws have clear proof that it is the saddle of Muhammadasws Bin Aliasws, and as for the mule, so weasws have bought it not so long ago and you know it, and what you are saying’.
en.sarwar: 48. From him, from Ahmad, from Al-Barqy, from Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Hammaad Bin usmaan who said: ‘Musa Bin Isa was in his house which was in the place of Sa’ee (nearby the Kaaba), overlooking it when he saw Abu Al-Hassan Musa (a.s.) coming from Al-Marwa upon his (a.s.) mule. So he ordered Ibn Hayyaaj, a man from Hamdaan who was very close to him (a.s.) to go and hold on to the reins and make a claim for the mule. So he came up to him (a.s.), held on to the reins, and staked a claim for the mule. Abu Al-Hassan (a.s.) took his (a.s.) feet off and dismounted from it and said to his (a.s.) servant: ‘Take the saddle and hand (the mule) over to him’. He said, ‘And the saddle is mine as well’. So Abu Al-Hassan (a.s.) said: ‘You are lying, for we (a.s.) have clear proof that it is the saddle of Muhammad (a.s.) Bin Ali (a.s.), and as for the mule, so we (a.s.) have bought it not so long ago and you know it, and what you are saying’.
